subject: [PATCH v2 8/9] nfsd: cap decoded POSIX ACL count to bound sort cost

2026-05-30.patch_summary: This patch fixes a performance issue in the NFS server by capping the number of POSIX ACL entries that can be decoded from a client request. The decoder previously allowed an unlimited number of entries, which led to unbounded CPU usage due to an O(n^2) bubble sort operation. The patch introduces a check to reject requests with more than the maximum allowed number of entries (NFS_ACL_MAX_ENTRIES), preventing excessive CPU usage and ensuring that ACLs set via NFSv4 POSIX ACL extensions are retrievable via NFSACL.
